items.summary: Perform a ground-up rewrite of the frontend-to-backend wiring. Replace the current actor initialization and query setup with a minimal, proven approach: (1) In useActor.ts, create the actor synchronously using HttpAgent with anonymous identity when no Internet Identity principal is present — never return null or undefined; log the resolved canister ID at init time so it is visible in the browser console. (2) In useQueries.ts, call useActor() at the top of every hook, assert the actor is non-null before passing it to React Query's queryFn, and surface the raw error message in the query's error state so failures are visible in the UI. (3) In App.tsx, ensure the provider order is exactly: ThemeProvider (outermost) → QueryClientProvider → InternetIdentityProvider → RouterProvider (innermost), with no hooks called outside of a valid provider context. (4) Remove or disable any code path that conditionally skips the getAllIncidents query (e.g., `enabled: !!actor` guards that evaluate to false for anonymous users). The query must run on every page load regardless of authentication state. (5) Confirm the Vite environment variable for the canister ID (VITE_CANISTER_ID_BACKEND or equivalent) is referenced consistently in one place and that the build config exposes it correctly.
